document.masterDocTitle: Contribution to the knowledge of the genus Dendarus Dejean, 1821 (Coleoptera: Tenebrionidae: Blaptinae: Dendarini) from Iran, Turkmenistan and some adjacent territories

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Contribution to the knowledge of the genus Dendarus Dejean, 1821 (Coleoptera: Tenebrionidae: Blaptinae: Dendarini) from Iran, Turkmenistan and some adjacent territories

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Precaspian Institute of Biological Resources of the Daghestan Federal Research Centre of the Russian Academy of Sciences, M. Gadzhiev str., 45, Makhachkala, Republic of Dagestan 367000, Russia. & Dagestan State University, M. Gadzhiev str., 43 a, Makhachkala, Republic of Dagestan 367000, Russia.
Museo Civico di Storia Naturale “ Giacomo Doria ”, Genova, 16121 Italy.

heading.emphasis:
Dendarus vagabundus vagabundus Reitter, 1904
, sp. resurr.

paragraph.treatmentCitationGroup:
=
D. transcaspicus medvedevi Kaszab, 1973: 55
, fig. 7, photo 16, syn. n.

paragraph.materialsCitation:
Holotype of
Dendarus vagans
, ♂ ( HNHM ): “Transcasp. Kisil-Aswat” [now Turkmenistan : Serdar ], “
transcaspicus Branczik
det. dr. Kaszab”, “
Dendarus vagans
i.l. m 1899”, “Typus
Dendarus vagans Reitt. Coll. Reitter
”.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 7.
Dendarus vagabundus
, male, holotype of
D. vagans
(junior homonym). A = habitus, laterally; B = habitus, dorsally; C = abdomen; D = dissected aedeagus and tergite VII; E = labels.

FIGURE 8.
D. vagabundus
, habitus. A = male, dorsally (Iran, North Khorasan Province: Bojnurd); B = male, ventrally (Turkmenistan, Kopetdag); C = female, dorsally (Turkmenistan, Kopetdag); D = male, dorsally (Iran, Golestan Prov.: Khoshyeylag); F = female, dorsally (the same locality).

FIGURE 9.
Dendarus spp.
, male protibia. A =
D. armeniacus
; B =
D. crenulatus
; C =
D. leonhardi
; D =
D. simplex
; E =
D. matthewsi
sp. n.
; F =
D. transcaspicus
; G =
D. vagabundus
.

FIGURE 10.
Dendarus spp.
, male tergite VIII. A =
D. armeniacus
; B =
D. crenulatus
; C =
D. leonhardi
; D =
D. matthewsi
sp. n.
; E =
D. transcaspicus
; F =
D. vagabundus
.

subSubSection.paragraph: Female. Body much more robust, protibiae weakly bent. Lateral margins of pronotum always widely emarginated in basal quarter; posterior angles acute, stronger protruding.

Notes.
Dendarus vagans Reitter, 1902
and
D. vagabundus Reitter, 1904
were considered by Kaszab (1973) as junior synonyms of
D. transcaspicus transcaspicus Brancsik, 1899
as a result of misidentification.

The holotype of
D. vagabundus medvedevi Kaszab, 1973
(studied by the first author in HNHM but not photographed) from Saramsakli (now Turkmenistan , Central Kopetdag, Sarymsakly spring, 38°29ʹ11ʺ N , 57°6ʹ49ʺ E ) is only a deviant wrinkled specimen of
D. vagabundus vagabundus
with a less protruding tooth on the inner side. So, we propose the following synonymy:
Dendarus vagabundus vagabundus Reitter, 1904
=
D. transcaspicus medvedevi Kaszab, 1973
, syn. n.
Dendarus vagabundus afghanicus Kaszab, 1973
from Central Afghanistan (Sang Mazar, Pur Chaman) is a clear subspecies, which differs from the nominotypical one by the deeper notch and right tooth on inner side of male protibia and denser and coarser puncturation of the pronotum.

Distribution. Iran (recorded for Iran by
Medvedev & Nepesova (1990 a )
under the name
D. armeniacus
), Turkmenistan .
